message:
feat(wasm): expose paneContextLost + paneRenderStalled bindings

Follow-up to a2e4c5dd (feat(pane-web): handle WebGPU context loss +
render stall interruptions, bd-30abw). That commit added the
RunnerCore::pane_context_lost / pane_render_stalled entry points
and the PaneCancelReason::ContextLost / RenderStalled lifecycle
variants, but the wasm surface in ftui-showcase-wasm never wired
the new variants through to JS:

- pane_dispatch_to_js()'s exhaustive match over
  ftui_web::pane_pointer_capture::PanePointerLifecyclePhase was
  still missing arms for ContextLost and RenderStalled. Without
  this commit, the crate would no longer compile on wasm32 once
  the parent commit landed — the match would fall through as
  non-exhaustive against the upstream enum.

- ShowcaseRunner exposed every other pane lifecycle hook to JS
  via #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = paneX)] but not the two new ones.
  Hosts using the showcase-wasm API therefore had no way to
  notify the runtime when the browser-side WebGPU context was
  lost or the render loop stalled.

Add the missing match arms (mapping ContextLost → "context_lost"
and RenderStalled → "render_stalled" so the string-coded phase
observed by JS matches the Rust-side format_pane_log_entry
convention from runner_core.rs exactly), plus two new wasm-bindgen
methods that forward to the underlying RunnerCore hooks:

- paneContextLost() calls self.inner.pane_context_lost() and
  threads the returned PaneDispatchSummary through
  pane_dispatch_with_state(_, None) so the JS host sees the same
  dispatch/log envelope it already handles for Cancel/Blur/etc.
- paneRenderStalled() does the same for pane_render_stalled().

Both use #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = …)] with camelCase to match the
surrounding convention (paneCapture, paneCaptureAcquired,
paneLostPointerCapture).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
